evals.description: PHP fatal error fix — iteration 1, verifies output schema and specificity

triage.reasoning: Error text contains PHP Fatal error in WooCommerce plugin file

suspected_causes.cause: WooCommerce plugin file references deprecated function removed in WC 8.0
suspected_causes.evidence: Error in WooCommerce core file on function deprecated in version 8.0

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: WooCommerce 8.x fatal error on deprecated function wc_get_price_including_tax() likely triggered by a third-party plugin still calling the removed function.

input.original_symptom: White screen on all pages including admin
input.original_error_text: Fatal error: Call to undefined function wc_get_price_including_tax() in /wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-product.php on line 247

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
fix_title is a non-empty string describing the fix
root_cause_summary is a non-empty string citing specific evidence (plugin name, file path, or error message)
fix_steps is an array of 2-6 entries
Each fix step has step_number, action, method, code_or_command, file_path, expected_result, safe_to_do_on_live, and rollback_instruction fields
method in each step is one of 'wp-admin', 'wp-cli', 'ftp', 'code', 'hosting-panel', or 'database'
action field in every step names a specific plugin, file path, menu location, or command — not a vague instruction
Any step with safe_to_do_on_live: false has a non-null rollback_instruction
verification_steps is an array with at least 2 entries describing observable outcomes
estimated_time_minutes is a positive integer
if_this_does_not_work is a non-null string naming a specific next diagnostic action
Output contains no prose outside the JSON structure

evals.description: Iteration 2 with validator rejection — revised fix addresses rejection reasons

triage.reasoning: PHP fatal error in plugin file

suspected_causes.cause: Plugin conflict between Advanced Custom Fields Pro and the theme's custom blocks
suspected_causes.evidence: Error triggered after ACF Pro update

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: ACF Pro update likely broke compatibility with theme's custom block registration.

input.original_symptom: Block editor crashes when trying to edit any page

validator_rejection_reasons.reason: Step 1 action says 'Deactivate the conflicting plugin' without naming the plugin
validator_rejection_reasons.suggestion: Name the specific plugin: 'Go to Plugins → Installed Plugins and deactivate Advanced Custom Fields Pro'

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
The revised fix addresses the specificity rejection — action in step 1 names 'Advanced Custom Fields Pro' or the specific plugin identified in the diagnostic
Does not repeat the generic 'Deactivate the conflicting plugin' action that was rejected
fix_steps[0].action contains a specific plugin name or specific menu path

evals.description: Performance issue fix — bottleneck-specific steps generated

triage.reasoning: User reports slow load times, TTFB over 2 seconds

suspected_causes.cause: High TTFB (2.4s) on shared hosting with no server-side caching detected
suspected_causes.evidence: PageSpeed TTFB 2.4s; no caching plugin signature found in HTML

diagnostic.primary_diagnosis: Server TTFB of 2.4s on shared hosting with no caching plugin — install a page caching plugin to reduce server response time.

input.original_symptom: Site takes over 5 seconds to load on mobile

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
root_cause_summary cites the TTFB metric (2.4s) or missing caching plugin from the diagnostic
fix_steps address the server_ttfb bottleneck — likely involves installing a caching plugin
verification_steps include a step to clear cache before testing
estimated_time_minutes is a positive integer
